notes: Confirmed: SKCanvasView and SKGLView in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor do not expose a public CanvasSize property. The canvasSize field is private in both components across all tested versions (2.88.9, 3.119.2, and main branch source). Every other platform view (WPF, WinUI, WindowsForms, GTK3, GTK4, macOS, iOS, Android, Tizen) exposes public SKSize CanvasSize. The Dpi property was already exposed publicly on the Blazor views via PR #1832, establishing the exact pattern requested. PR #1912 attempted this fix but was closed without merge.

reproductionSteps.description: Investigate main branch source: confirm canvasSize is still private in both Blazor view components
Verify other platform views expose public CanvasSize — confirming the parity gap
Verify Dpi property IS already exposed on Blazor views — confirming the pattern from PR #1832 that should be replicated

versionResults.notes: CS1061: SKCanvasView does not contain a definition for CanvasSize
CS1061: SKCanvasView does not contain a definition for CanvasSize — same gap in latest stable
canvasSize field remains private in both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs on main branch

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with trivial implementation. The pattern is established (Dpi property, 25+ other platform views), and PR #1912 demonstrated the exact fix. Needs maintainer decision on whether to accept.

actions.description: Link to closed PR #1912 that attempted this fix

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the suggestion! We investigated and can confirm that the `CanvasSize` property is not currently available on the Blazor views (`SKCanvasView` and `SKGLView`).

**Investigation:**
- The `canvasSize` field exists as a private field in both `SKCanvasView.razor.cs` and `SKGLView.razor.cs`, but is not exposed publicly.
- The `Dpi` property was already exposed via PR #1832 using the exact same pattern (`public double Dpi => dpi;`), so the precedent exists.
- Every other platform view (WPF, WinUI, WindowsForms, GTK3, GTK4, macOS, iOS, Android, Tizen) exposes a public `CanvasSize` property.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 2.88.9 | ❌ CanvasSize not available |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | ❌ CanvasSize not available |
| main (source) | ❌ Still private |

**Workaround:** Inside your `OnPaintSurface` handler, you can access the canvas dimensions via `e.Info.Size`:

```csharp
void OnPaintSurface(SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs e)
{
    var size = e.Info.Size; // SKSizeI with Width and Height
}
```

This only works within the paint callback, not for external layout logic.

workarounds: Inside OnPaintSurface handler, use e.Info.Size to get the canvas dimensions (pixel size). This only works within the paint callback, not for external layout logic.
